working_definition: A late B cell precursor identified in fetal skin, characterized by expression of the pre-B cell receptor (surrogate light chain paired with mu heavy chain) and cytoplasmic IgM. Pre-B cells are detected in fetal skin from as early as 7-8 PCW in related fetal haematopoiesis studies.

best_match.cl_definition: A precursor B cell is a B cell with the phenotype CD19-positive.

justification: CL:0000817 'precursor B cell' (also known as pre-B cell) is an exact match for the 'Pre B cell' annotation in the fetal skin atlas. The CL term represents the developmental stage at which the pre-BCR is expressed and light chain rearrangement is initiated. The atlas annotation corresponds to this well-established B cell developmental stage.

other_candidates.cl_definition: A large pre-B-II cell expressing the pre-BCR and undergoing clonal expansion.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: Too specific: the atlas annotation 'Pre B cell' likely encompasses both large and small pre-B cell stages.

other_candidates.cl_definition: A pre-B-I cell at the earliest pre-B stage.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: Too specific: the atlas does not distinguish pre-B-I from pre-B-II stages.

recommendation: Use CL:0000817 (precursor B cell). This is the standard CL term for the pre-B cell stage.
